prompt: Tight three-quarter detail of two cabinet door samples leaning against a piece of butcher-block on a workbench. Left sample: an obvious thermofoil-laminate cabinet door with a faux wood-grain pattern, slightly uneven sheen, a peeling corner edge revealing the MDF underneath. Right sample: a solid maple shaker-style cabinet door, hand-sanded edges, painted in warm white with a brushed-brass cup pull installed. Real saw dust on the bench. Shot with a 100mm macro lens at a tight three-quarter angle, single warm tungsten work-lamp casting a directional pool of light from the left, dramatic falloff into shadow. Slightly desaturated palette, NOT HGTV bright. A small red mechanical pencil in the foreground as the only saturated color. Working-contractor catalog feel. No readable text, no logos.
